# Teal design system - full documentation

Version: v0.3.0
---

# Getting started

Teal provides typed React modules, semantic design tokens, and tested interaction behavior for Kryv applications.

## Principles

- **Quiet by default** - Surface, type, and spacing create hierarchy. Color is reserved for action and meaning.

- **Keyboard complete** - Every interactive module has visible focus and a complete keyboard path.

- **One supported interface** - Applications import typed modules and compiled styles from @kryv/teal.

- **Recipes before abstractions** - Product patterns remain recipes until repeated behavior proves a stable module seam.

## Installation

### 1. Install the package

React 18 or 19 is required as a peer dependency.

```bash
npm install @kryv/teal
```

```bash
pnpm add @kryv/teal
```

```bash
yarn add @kryv/teal
```

```bash
bun add @kryv/teal
```

### 2. Import the styles

Import the compiled component styles once at your app entry. Teal modules do not require Tailwind in the consuming application.

```js
import '@kryv/teal/styles.css'
```

### 3. Optionally apply document defaults

base.css applies Teal typography, body colors, selection, and scrollbar defaults. Skip it when the application already owns document-level styling.

```js
import '@kryv/teal/base.css'
```

### 4. Optionally configure Tailwind 3

Use the preset only when application markup needs Teal semantic utilities such as bg-surface-container or text-on-surface-variant.

```js
import tealPreset from '@kryv/teal/tailwind-preset'

/** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */
export default {
  ...tealPreset,
  content: ['./index.html', './src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'],
}
```

### 5. Load the fonts

Teal typesets headlines in Plus Jakarta Sans and body copy in Manrope. Add the Google Fonts links to your document head.

```html
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin />
<link
  href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Manrope:wght@400;500;600;700;800&family=Plus+Jakarta+Sans:wght@500;600;700;800&display=swap"
  rel="stylesheet"
/>
```

### 6. Enable dark mode

Tokens switch themes when the dark class is present on the html element. Persist the choice however your app prefers.

```js
// Teal themes by toggling the "dark" class on <html>.
document.documentElement.classList.toggle('dark', darkMode)
```

### 7. Build something

```jsx
import { Button, Field, Input } from '@kryv/teal'

export function SignInForm() {
  return (
    <form className="grid gap-4">
      <Field label="Email" required>
        <Input type="email" />
      </Field>
      <Button type="submit">Sign in</Button>
    </form>
  )
}
```

---

# Foundations

Semantic tokens keep visual decisions consistent across themes and products.

## Color

Use semantic roles instead of raw palette values.

- **Primary** - `--color-primary`
- **Success** - `--color-tertiary`
- **Warning** - `--color-warning`
- **Danger** - `--color-error`
- **Surface** - `--color-surface-container`
- **Elevated surface** - `--color-surface-container-high`

## Typography

- **Plus Jakarta Sans** - `--teal-font-headline`
- **Manrope** - `--teal-font-body`

## Shape, elevation, and motion

- Controls use 12 to 16 pixel radii.
- Surfaces use soft teal elevation.
- Motion stays between 150 and 200 milliseconds and respects reduced motion.

## Visual tokens

Override these supported CSS properties at `:root` to tune Teal without rewriting module styles.

- **Control radius** - `--teal-radius-control`
- **Surface radius** - `--teal-radius-surface`
- **Pill radius** - `--teal-radius-pill`
- **Subtle border** - `--teal-border-subtle`
- **Strong border** - `--teal-border-strong`
- **Focus ring** - `--teal-focus-ring`
- **Raised elevation** - `--teal-shadow-raised`
- **Overlay elevation** - `--teal-shadow-overlay`
- **Extra-small icon** - `--teal-icon-xs`
- **Small icon** - `--teal-icon-sm`
- **Medium icon** - `--teal-icon-md`
- **Large icon** - `--teal-icon-lg`
- **Extra-large icon** - `--teal-icon-xl`
- **Fast motion** - `--teal-motion-fast`
- **Standard motion** - `--teal-motion-standard`

---

# Button

Actions with consistent hierarchy, sizing, loading, and accessible icon treatment.

## Usage

```js
import { Button, IconButton } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Button variant="primary">Save changes</Button>
<IconButton label="More options"><MoreHorizontal /></IconButton>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

A user needs to take an explicit action.
### Avoid when

The control is only communicating status or navigation.
### Behavior

Loading disables the native button until the action completes.
### Responsive

Let actions wrap in narrow toolbars instead of shrinking their labels.

## Examples

### Variants and sizes

Primary, secondary, ghost, and danger variants with a dedicated IconButton for icon-only actions.

```jsx
import { Button, IconButton } from '@kryv/teal'
import { Search } from 'lucide-react'

export function ButtonDemo() {
  return (
    <>
      <Button size="sm">Small</Button>
      <Button>Primary</Button>
      <Button variant="secondary">Secondary</Button>
      <Button variant="ghost">Ghost</Button>
      <Button variant="danger">Danger</Button>
      <Button loading>Saving</Button>
      <IconButton label="Search">
        <Search />
      </IconButton>
    </>
  )
}
```

### Disabled actions

Use disabled state when the action cannot be completed yet, and explain why nearby.

```jsx
import { Button, IconButton } from '@kryv/teal'
import { Search } from 'lucide-react'

export function ButtonDemo() {
  return (
    <>
      <Button size="sm">Small</Button>
      <Button>Primary</Button>
      <Button variant="secondary">Secondary</Button>
      <Button variant="ghost">Ghost</Button>
      <Button variant="danger">Danger</Button>
      <Button loading>Saving</Button>
      <IconButton label="Search">
        <Search />
      </IconButton>
    </>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Button

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `variant` | `"danger" \| "primary" \| "secondary" \| "ghost" \| null` | `"primary"` | Visual hierarchy of the action. |
| `size` | `"sm" \| "md" \| "lg" \| null` | `"md"` | Height and horizontal padding. |
| `asChild` | `boolean` | `false` | Merges the component onto its child element instead of rendering the default element (Radix Slot). Cannot be combined with disabled or loading. |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables the button, prepends a spinner, and sets aria-busy. |

### IconButton

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `label` (required) | `string` | - | Accessible label applied as `aria-label`; also used as the default tooltip text. |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `false` | Replaces the icon with a spinner and disables the button while true. |
| `variant` | `"danger" \| "secondary" \| "ghost" \| null` | `"ghost"` | Visual treatment of the icon button. |
| `size` | `"sm" \| "md" \| "lg" \| null` | `"md"` | Hit-target size; the icon scales with it. |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Tab` | Moves focus to and from the button. |
| `Enter` + `Space` | Activates the button. |

- Icon-only actions use IconButton, which requires a label applied as aria-label and mirrored in the title tooltip.
- Loading buttons set aria-busy and are disabled so they cannot be activated twice.
- A loading IconButton replaces its icon with a spinner while keeping the accessible label.
- The danger variant carries meaning through its label as well as its color.

---

# Field

A deep form seam that connects labels, descriptions, errors, and required state.

## Usage

```js
import { Field, Input } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Field label="Display name" description="Shown to other workspace members" required>
  <Input defaultValue="Avery Chen" />
</Field>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

A control needs a visible label, help text, or validation message.
### Avoid when

The control already owns an equivalent form-label composition.
### Behavior

Field provides the id and ARIA relationships consumed by its child control.
### Responsive

Keep labels readable and let long error messages wrap.

## Examples

### Label, description, and error

Field wires the label, help text, and error message to the control inside it automatically.

```jsx
import { Field, Input, TextArea } from '@kryv/teal'

export function FieldDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-md gap-5">
      <Field label="Display name" description="Shown to other workspace members" required>
        <Input defaultValue="Avery Chen" />
      </Field>
      <Field label="Notes" error="Keep notes under 240 characters">
        <TextArea defaultValue="Notify the incident team before changing this account." />
      </Field>
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Account profile

Pair a required profile value with a clear validation message.

```jsx
import { Field, Input, TextArea } from '@kryv/teal'

export function FieldDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-md gap-5">
      <Field label="Display name" description="Shown to other workspace members" required>
        <Input defaultValue="Avery Chen" />
      </Field>
      <Field label="Notes" error="Keep notes under 240 characters">
        <TextArea defaultValue="Notify the incident team before changing this account." />
      </Field>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Field

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` (required) | `ReactNode` | - | The form control this field labels and describes. |
| `description` | `ReactNode` | - | Help text rendered below the control and linked to it for assistive technology. |
| `error` | `ReactNode` | - | Error message; marks the control as invalid and links the message to it. |
| `label` (required) | `ReactNode` | - | Visible label associated with the control. |
| `required` | `boolean` | `false` | Marks the control as required and renders a required indicator. |
| `invalid` | `boolean` | - | Marks the control invalid without requiring a visible error message. |

### Label

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `asChild` | `boolean` | - | Merges the component onto its child element instead of rendering the default element (Radix Slot). |

## Accessibility

- The label is wired to the control with htmlFor, so clicking the label focuses the control.
- Description and error text are linked to Input and TextArea children through aria-describedby automatically.
- Setting error marks the control aria-invalid and renders the message in error color.
- The required indicator is decorative; the underlying control keeps the native required attribute.
- Custom controls can join the same wiring through the exported useFieldControl hook and mergeDescriptionIds helper.

---

# Input and TextArea

Native text controls with Teal sizing, invalid states, and forwarded refs.

## Usage

```js
import { Input, TextArea } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Input placeholder="Project name" />
<TextArea placeholder="Notes" rows={4} />
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

Users enter or search for short text.
### Avoid when

A constrained set of choices or a long-form editor is clearer.
### Behavior

Native input behavior is preserved, including browser validation and refs.
### Responsive

Use full width on small screens and constrain width at larger sizes.

## Examples

### States

Default, invalid, and disabled inputs share the same sizing and focus treatment.

```jsx
import { Input, TextArea } from '@kryv/teal'

export function InputDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-md gap-4">
      <Input aria-label="Search projects" placeholder="Search projects" />
      <Input aria-label="Email address" defaultValue="not-an-email" aria-invalid="true" />
      <Input aria-label="Disabled input" placeholder="Disabled input" disabled />
      <TextArea aria-label="Team update" placeholder="Write a short update for the team" />
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Search and inline validation

Use a compact search control alongside an input that reports its invalid state.

```jsx
import { Input, TextArea } from '@kryv/teal'

export function InputDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-md gap-4">
      <Input aria-label="Search projects" placeholder="Search projects" />
      <Input aria-label="Email address" defaultValue="not-an-email" aria-invalid="true" />
      <Input aria-label="Disabled input" placeholder="Disabled input" disabled />
      <TextArea aria-label="Team update" placeholder="Write a short update for the team" />
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Input

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `size` | `"sm" \| "md" \| "lg" \| null` | `"md"` | Height and text size of the control. |

### TextArea

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `size` | `"sm" \| "md" \| "lg" \| null` | `"md"` | Text size of the control. |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Tab` | Moves focus to and from the control. |
| `Typing` | Edits the value using native text editing keys. |

- Inputs are native elements, so autofill, spellcheck, and input modes work without configuration.
- Inside a Field, aria-invalid and aria-describedby are applied automatically when the field has an error or description.

---

# Select

An accessible single-value picker with keyboard navigation, typeahead, and collision-aware positioning.

## Usage

```js
import { Select } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Select
  aria-label="Role"
  defaultValue="viewer"
  options={[
    { value: 'admin', label: 'Administrator' },
    { value: 'viewer', label: 'Viewer' },
  ]}
/>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

Users choose one value from a known list.
### Avoid when

There are only two choices or users need to compare all options at once.
### Behavior

Radix manages keyboard navigation, typeahead, focus, and collision handling.
### Responsive

The trigger fills its parent width and the menu follows its measured width.

## Examples

### Controlled selection

Select is controlled through value and onValueChange with an options array.

```jsx
import { useState } from 'react'
import { Select } from '@kryv/teal'

export function SelectDemo() {
  const [role, setRole] = useState('viewer')
  return (
    <div className="w-full max-w-xs">
      <Select
        aria-label="Project role"
        value={role}
        onValueChange={setRole}
        options={[
          { value: 'admin', label: 'Administrator' },
          { value: 'editor', label: 'Editor' },
          { value: 'viewer', label: 'Viewer' },
        ]}
      />
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Role assignment

Use a labeled picker when a person must choose one role.

```jsx
import { useState } from 'react'
import { Select } from '@kryv/teal'

export function SelectDemo() {
  const [role, setRole] = useState('viewer')
  return (
    <div className="w-full max-w-xs">
      <Select
        aria-label="Project role"
        value={role}
        onValueChange={setRole}
        options={[
          { value: 'admin', label: 'Administrator' },
          { value: 'editor', label: 'Editor' },
          { value: 'viewer', label: 'Viewer' },
        ]}
      />
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Keyboard selection

Typeahead and arrow-key navigation keep long option lists efficient.

```jsx
import { useState } from 'react'
import { Select } from '@kryv/teal'

export function SelectDemo() {
  const [role, setRole] = useState('viewer')
  return (
    <div className="w-full max-w-xs">
      <Select
        aria-label="Project role"
        value={role}
        onValueChange={setRole}
        options={[
          { value: 'admin', label: 'Administrator' },
          { value: 'editor', label: 'Editor' },
          { value: 'viewer', label: 'Viewer' },
        ]}
      />
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Select

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `aria-describedby` | `string` | - | Id of an element that describes the select, forwarded to the trigger. |
| `aria-label` | `string` | - | Accessible name when there is no visible label. |
| `aria-invalid` | `boolean \| "true" \| "false"` | - | Marks the trigger invalid for form validation and screen readers. |
| `id` | `string` | - | Explicit id; otherwise Field or an internal id is used. |
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional classes merged onto the trigger. |
| `options` (required) | `SelectOption[]` | - | Options rendered in the listbox. |
| `placeholder` | `ReactNode` | `Select an option` | Text shown when no value is selected. |
| `size` | `enum` | - | Size of the trigger button. |
| `defaultValue` | `string` | - | - |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: string) => void` | - | - |
| `value` | `string` | - | - |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Enter` + `Space` + `Arrow Down` | Opens the listbox and highlights the selected option. |
| `Arrow Up` + `Arrow Down` | Moves the highlight between options. |
| `Home` + `End` | Highlights the first or last option. |
| `Typing` | Typeahead jumps to the first matching option. |
| `Enter` + `Tab` | Commits the highlighted option and closes the listbox. |
| `Escape` | Closes the listbox without changing the value. |

- Provide aria-label when the select has no visible label; the demo options render visible text either way.
- Disabled options are skipped during keyboard navigation and typeahead.

---

# Checkbox

Boolean and indeterminate selection with an integrated label and description.

## Usage

```js
import { Checkbox } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Checkbox label="Include archived projects" defaultChecked />
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

Users can select independent items or a tri-state group.
### Avoid when

Changing the value should take effect immediately as a setting.
### Behavior

Checked, unchecked, and indeterminate states remain native and form-friendly.
### Responsive

Allow supporting text to wrap beside the control.

## Examples

### Checked, indeterminate, and disabled

Checkbox supports a tri-state checked prop for select-all patterns.

```jsx
import { Checkbox } from '@kryv/teal'

export function CheckboxDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-sm gap-4">
      <Checkbox
        label="Include archived projects"
        description="Show projects that have been archived"
        defaultChecked
      />
      <Checkbox label="Select all reports" defaultChecked="indeterminate" />
      <Checkbox label="Share with external reviewers" />
      <Checkbox label="Billing locked" description="Managed by your administrator" disabled />
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Bulk selection

Use indeterminate state when a table selection contains both checked and unchecked rows.

```jsx
import { useState } from 'react'
import { Checkbox } from '@kryv/teal'

const rows = [
  { id: 'orion', name: 'Project Orion', owner: 'Avery Chen' },
  { id: 'atlas', name: 'Project Atlas', owner: 'Morgan Lee' },
  { id: 'nova', name: 'Project Nova', owner: 'Riley Singh' },
]

export function CheckboxBulkDemo() {
  const [selected, setSelected] = useState(['orion'])
  const allSelected = selected.length === rows.length
  const partiallySelected = selected.length > 0 && !allSelected
  function toggle(id) {
    setSelected((current) => current.includes(id) ? current.filter((value) => value !== id) : [...current, id])
  }
  return (
    <div className="w-full max-w-xl overflow-hidden rounded-2xl border border-outline-variant/30">
      <div className="flex items-center gap-3 border-b border-outline-variant/30 bg-surface-container-high px-4 py-3">
        <Checkbox label="Select all projects" visuallyHiddenLabel checked={allSelected ? true : partiallySelected ? 'indeterminate' : false} onCheckedChange={(value) => setSelected(value ? rows.map((row) => row.id) : [])} />
        <span className="text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-on-surface-variant">Project</span>
        <span className="ml-auto text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-on-surface-variant">Owner</span>
      </div>
      {rows.map((row) => (
        <div key={row.id} className="flex items-center gap-3 border-b border-outline-variant/20 bg-surface-container px-4 py-3 last:border-b-0">
          <Checkbox label={row.name} visuallyHiddenLabel checked={selected.includes(row.id)} onCheckedChange={() => toggle(row.id)} />
          <span className="text-sm text-on-surface">{row.name}</span>
          <span className="ml-auto text-sm text-on-surface-variant">{row.owner}</span>
        </div>
      ))}
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Permission groups

Group independent permissions under one clear label and description.

```jsx
import { Checkbox } from '@kryv/teal'

export function CheckboxDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-sm gap-4">
      <Checkbox
        label="Include archived projects"
        description="Show projects that have been archived"
        defaultChecked
      />
      <Checkbox label="Select all reports" defaultChecked="indeterminate" />
      <Checkbox label="Share with external reviewers" />
      <Checkbox label="Billing locked" description="Managed by your administrator" disabled />
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Checkbox

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `description` | `ReactNode` | - | Supporting text rendered below the label. |
| `label` | `ReactNode` | - | Visible label rendered next to the checkbox. Required outside a Field; inside a Field the Field's label is used. |
| `visuallyHiddenLabel` | `boolean` | `false` | Hides the label visually while keeping it available to screen readers. |
| `asChild` | `boolean` | - | Merges the component onto its child element instead of rendering the default element (Radix Slot). |
| `checked` | `boolean \| "indeterminate"` | - | Controlled checked state. Use "indeterminate" for select-all patterns. |
| `defaultChecked` | `boolean \| "indeterminate"` | - | Initial checked state for uncontrolled usage. |
| `onCheckedChange` | `(checked: CheckedState) => void` | - | Called with the next state when the user toggles the checkbox. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | - | Prevents interaction and dims the control. |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Tab` | Moves focus to and from the checkbox. |
| `Space` | Toggles the checkbox; indeterminate moves to checked. |

- Supports a tri-state checked value for select-all patterns, announced as "mixed" by screen readers.
- The label is clickable and the description is linked through aria-describedby.
- Inside a Field, the Field label is the only rendered label; pass label only for standalone use.

---

# Switch

An immediate boolean setting with explicit labeling and controlled or uncontrolled state.

## Usage

```js
import { Switch } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Switch label="Security notifications" description="High-risk account activity" defaultChecked />
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

A boolean setting takes effect immediately.
### Avoid when

The user must submit several values together as a form.
### Behavior

The label and description remain associated with the switch control.
### Responsive

Keep the control at a fixed size while the setting copy takes available width.

## Examples

### Settings

Switches apply immediately, so label them as settings rather than form fields.

```jsx
import { Switch } from '@kryv/teal'

export function SwitchDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-sm gap-5">
      <Switch
        label="Security notifications"
        description="Receive alerts for high-risk account activity"
        defaultChecked
      />
      <Switch label="Weekly digest" />
      <Switch label="Auto-deploy previews" size="sm" defaultChecked />
      <Switch label="Billing alerts" description="Managed by your administrator" disabled />
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Application settings

Use switches in a settings list for changes that apply immediately.

```jsx
import { Switch } from '@kryv/teal'

export function SwitchDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-sm gap-5">
      <Switch
        label="Security notifications"
        description="Receive alerts for high-risk account activity"
        defaultChecked
      />
      <Switch label="Weekly digest" />
      <Switch label="Auto-deploy previews" size="sm" defaultChecked />
      <Switch label="Billing alerts" description="Managed by your administrator" disabled />
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Compact settings

The small size keeps dense preference lists scannable without losing the accessible label.

```jsx
import { Switch } from '@kryv/teal'

export function SwitchDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-sm gap-5">
      <Switch
        label="Security notifications"
        description="Receive alerts for high-risk account activity"
        defaultChecked
      />
      <Switch label="Weekly digest" />
      <Switch label="Auto-deploy previews" size="sm" defaultChecked />
      <Switch label="Billing alerts" description="Managed by your administrator" disabled />
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Switch

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `description` | `ReactNode` | - | Supporting text rendered below the label. |
| `label` | `ReactNode` | - | Visible label describing the setting the switch controls. Required outside a Field; inside a Field the Field's label is used. |
| `size` | `enum` | `md` | Track and thumb size. |
| `asChild` | `boolean` | - | Merges the component onto its child element instead of rendering the default element (Radix Slot). |
| `checked` | `boolean` | - | Controlled on/off state. |
| `defaultChecked` | `boolean` | - | Initial state for uncontrolled usage. |
| `onCheckedChange` | `(checked: boolean) => void` | - | Called with the next state when the user toggles the switch. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | - | Prevents interaction and dims the control. |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Tab` | Moves focus to and from the switch. |
| `Space` + `Enter` | Toggles the setting. |

- Renders role="switch" with aria-checked, so assistive technology announces it as an on/off setting.
- The label and description are wired to the control automatically.
- Inside a Field, the Field label is the only rendered label; pass label only for standalone use.

---

# Card

A structural surface for related content without ambiguous interactive behavior.

## Usage

```js
import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, CardFooter } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Card>
  <CardHeader>
    <CardTitle>Security report</CardTitle>
    <CardDescription>Updated five minutes ago</CardDescription>
  </CardHeader>
  <CardContent>No critical findings were detected.</CardContent>
</Card>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

Related content needs a structural surface.
### Avoid when

A card is being used only to decorate every section or hide a primary action.
### Behavior

Card is non-interactive by default and accepts an explicit polymorphic element.
### Responsive

Use compact padding and let card content define its width.

## Examples

### Composition

Cards compose header, content, and footer regions with consistent rhythm.

```jsx
import { Button, Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle } from '@kryv/teal'

export function CardDemo() {
  return (
    <Card className="w-full max-w-md">
      <CardHeader className="flex-col items-start justify-start gap-1.5">
        <CardTitle>Security report</CardTitle>
        <CardDescription>Updated five minutes ago</CardDescription>
      </CardHeader>
      <CardContent>
        <p className="text-sm text-on-surface">No critical findings were detected.</p>
      </CardContent>
      <CardFooter>
        <Button size="sm" variant="secondary">
          View report
        </Button>
      </CardFooter>
    </Card>
  )
}
```

### Report summary

Use a card to group a short summary and one related action.

```jsx
import { Button, Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle } from '@kryv/teal'

export function CardDemo() {
  return (
    <Card className="w-full max-w-md">
      <CardHeader className="flex-col items-start justify-start gap-1.5">
        <CardTitle>Security report</CardTitle>
        <CardDescription>Updated five minutes ago</CardDescription>
      </CardHeader>
      <CardContent>
        <p className="text-sm text-on-surface">No critical findings were detected.</p>
      </CardContent>
      <CardFooter>
        <Button size="sm" variant="secondary">
          View report
        </Button>
      </CardFooter>
    </Card>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Card

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Applies disabled styling and blocks interaction on interactive cards. |
| `type` | `enum` | - | Button type used when the card renders an interactive element. |
| `as` | `ElementType` | `div` | - |

### CardHeader

_This module forwards native element properties._

### CardTitle

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `titleAs` | `enum` | `"h2"` | Heading element used for the title; defaults to 'h2'. Adjust to fit the page outline. |

### CardDescription

_This module forwards native element properties._

### CardContent

_This module forwards native element properties._

### CardFooter

_This module forwards native element properties._

## Accessibility

- Cards are passive surfaces with no keyboard behavior of their own.
- Render real links and buttons inside a card rather than making the card itself clickable.
- The as prop lets you render the card as a section, article, or list item to fit the page outline.
- Disabled cards are dimmed, skip pointer events, and set aria-disabled.
- CardTitle renders an h2 by default; set titleAs so card headings fit the surrounding page outline.

---

# Badge

A compact semantic status indicator using canonical information variants.

## Usage

```js
import { Badge } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Badge variant="success">Deployed</Badge>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

A short status or category needs quick visual scanning.
### Avoid when

The content needs an action or a sentence of explanation.
### Behavior

Variant changes meaning without changing the content semantics.
### Responsive

Keep labels short so badges do not dominate dense rows.

## Examples

### Variants

Five variants cover neutral, informational, success, warning, and danger statuses.

```jsx
import { Badge } from '@kryv/teal'

export function BadgeDemo() {
  return (
    <>
      <Badge>Neutral</Badge>
      <Badge variant="info">Information</Badge>
      <Badge variant="success">Ready</Badge>
      <Badge variant="warning">Attention</Badge>
      <Badge variant="danger">Action required</Badge>
    </>
  )
}
```

### Table statuses

Keep status text explicit when badges appear in dense data rows.

```jsx
import { Badge } from '@kryv/teal'

export function BadgeDemo() {
  return (
    <>
      <Badge>Neutral</Badge>
      <Badge variant="info">Information</Badge>
      <Badge variant="success">Ready</Badge>
      <Badge variant="warning">Attention</Badge>
      <Badge variant="danger">Action required</Badge>
    </>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Badge

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `variant` | `"neutral" \| "info" \| "success" \| "warning" \| "danger" \| null` | `"neutral"` | Semantic color treatment. |

## Accessibility

- Badges are plain text, so their status is announced verbatim by screen readers.
- Always pair a variant with meaningful text; never rely on color alone to carry the status.

---

# Accordion

A stacked disclosure list with single and multi-open modes driven by a compact item interface.

## Usage

```js
import { Accordion } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Accordion
  defaultValue="sign-in"
  items={[
    { value: 'sign-in', title: 'Sign-in notifications', content: 'Get alerted when a new device signs in.' },
    { value: 'sessions', title: 'Active sessions', content: 'Review and revoke sessions.' },
  ]}
/>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

Sections of related content should be progressively disclosed.
### Avoid when

All content must be visible at once or sections are compared side by side.
### Behavior

Single mode keeps at most one item open and is collapsible; multiple mode opens any number.
### Responsive

Keep titles short so triggers stay on one line.

## Examples

### Single-open

At most one item is open, and the open item can be collapsed again.

```jsx
import { Accordion } from '@kryv/teal'

const securityItems = [
  {
    value: 'sign-in',
    title: 'Sign-in notifications',
    content: 'Get alerted when a new device signs in to your account.',
  },
  {
    value: 'sessions',
    title: 'Active sessions',
    content: 'Review and revoke sessions from the security page.',
  },
  {
    value: 'recovery',
    title: 'Recovery keys',
    content: 'Store recovery keys somewhere safe; each key bypasses two-factor sign-in once.',
    disabled: true,
  },
]

export function AccordionDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  if (exampleIndex === 1) {
    return <Accordion multiple defaultValue={['sign-in']} items={securityItems} className="w-full max-w-lg" />
  }
  return <Accordion defaultValue="sign-in" items={securityItems} className="w-full max-w-lg" />
}
```

### Multi-open and disabled

multiple allows any number of open items; disabled items cannot be toggled.

```jsx
import { Accordion } from '@kryv/teal'

const securityItems = [
  {
    value: 'sign-in',
    title: 'Sign-in notifications',
    content: 'Get alerted when a new device signs in to your account.',
  },
  {
    value: 'sessions',
    title: 'Active sessions',
    content: 'Review and revoke sessions from the security page.',
  },
  {
    value: 'recovery',
    title: 'Recovery keys',
    content: 'Store recovery keys somewhere safe; each key bypasses two-factor sign-in once.',
    disabled: true,
  },
]

export function AccordionDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  if (exampleIndex === 1) {
    return <Accordion multiple defaultValue={['sign-in']} items={securityItems} className="w-full max-w-lg" />
  }
  return <Accordion defaultValue="sign-in" items={securityItems} className="w-full max-w-lg" />
}
```

## Interface

### Accordion

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `className` | `string` | - | - |
| `items` (required) | `AccordionItem[]` | - | Items rendered by the accordion ({ value, title, content, disabled? }). |
| `multiple` | `boolean` | `false` | Multi-open mode. value, defaultValue, and onValueChange are typed string in single mode and string[] when multiple is true. |
| `defaultValue` | `string \| string[]` | - | - |
| `onValueChange` | `((value: string) => void) \| ((value: string[]) => void)` | - | - |
| `value` | `string \| string[]` | - | - |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Enter` + `Space` | Toggles the focused item. |
| `Arrow Up` + `Arrow Down` | Moves focus between item triggers. |
| `Home` + `End` | Focuses the first or last trigger. |

- Implements the ARIA accordion pattern through the Radix primitive, with state exposed as aria-expanded on each trigger.
- Disabled items are skipped and cannot be toggled.
- The chevron is decorative and hidden from assistive technology.

---

# Dialog

A modal surface that owns focus management, naming, dismissal, and scroll locking.

## Usage

```js
import { Dialog } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)

<Button onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>Open dialog</Button>
<Dialog
  open={open}
  onOpenChange={setOpen}
  title="Archive project?"
  description="The project can be restored later."
>
  <p>Project Orion will leave the active workspace.</p>
</Dialog>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

A decision or focused task must temporarily block the page.
### Avoid when

The content can be inline or handled by a popover.
### Behavior

Focus is trapped, Escape dismisses, and focus returns to the trigger.
### Responsive

Use the built-in size and allow the body to scroll inside the panel.

## Examples

### Confirmation

Dialog traps focus, restores it on close, and dismisses with Escape or the scrim.

```jsx
import { useState } from 'react'
import { Button, Dialog } from '@kryv/teal'

export function DialogDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
  return (
    <>
      <Button onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>{exampleIndex ? 'Open confirmation dialog' : 'Open dialog'}</Button>
      <Dialog
        open={open}
        onOpenChange={setOpen}
        title="Archive project?"
        description="The project can be restored later."
        footer={
          <>
            <Button variant="secondary" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>
              Cancel
            </Button>
            <Button variant="danger" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>
              Archive
            </Button>
          </>
        }
      >
        <p className="text-sm text-on-surface-variant">
          Project Orion and its reports will leave the active workspace.
        </p>
      </Dialog>
    </>
  )
}
```

### Destructive confirmation

Use a danger action only when the consequence is clear and reversible where possible.

```jsx
import { useState } from 'react'
import { Button, Dialog } from '@kryv/teal'

export function DialogDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
  return (
    <>
      <Button onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>{exampleIndex ? 'Open confirmation dialog' : 'Open dialog'}</Button>
      <Dialog
        open={open}
        onOpenChange={setOpen}
        title="Archive project?"
        description="The project can be restored later."
        footer={
          <>
            <Button variant="secondary" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>
              Cancel
            </Button>
            <Button variant="danger" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>
              Archive
            </Button>
          </>
        }
      >
        <p className="text-sm text-on-surface-variant">
          Project Orion and its reports will leave the active workspace.
        </p>
      </Dialog>
    </>
  )
}
```

### Long-form task

For focused tasks, keep the title visible and let the dialog body own its scroll.

```jsx
import { useState } from 'react'
import { Button, Dialog } from '@kryv/teal'

export function DialogDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
  return (
    <>
      <Button onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>{exampleIndex ? 'Open confirmation dialog' : 'Open dialog'}</Button>
      <Dialog
        open={open}
        onOpenChange={setOpen}
        title="Archive project?"
        description="The project can be restored later."
        footer={
          <>
            <Button variant="secondary" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>
              Cancel
            </Button>
            <Button variant="danger" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>
              Archive
            </Button>
          </>
        }
      >
        <p className="text-sm text-on-surface-variant">
          Project Orion and its reports will leave the active workspace.
        </p>
      </Dialog>
    </>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Dialog

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` (required) | `ReactNode` | - | Body content of the dialog. |
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional classes merged onto the dialog panel. |
| `closeLabel` | `string` | `Close` | Accessible label for the close button. |
| `defaultOpen` | `boolean` | - | Initial open state when uncontrolled. |
| `description` | `ReactNode` | - | Supporting text rendered under the title. |
| `footer` | `ReactNode` | - | Action area rendered at the bottom of the dialog. |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void` | - | Called when the dialog opens or closes. |
| `open` | `boolean` | - | Controlled open state. |
| `restoreFocusRef` | `RefObject<HTMLElement \| null>` | - | Element that receives focus after a controlled dialog closes. |
| `size` | `enum` | `md` | Width of the dialog surface. |
| `title` (required) | `ReactNode` | - | Title rendered at the top; also the accessible name of the dialog. |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Escape` | Closes the dialog and returns focus to the trigger. |
| `Tab` + `Shift+Tab` | Cycles focus through the focusable elements inside the dialog. |

- Renders role="dialog" with aria-modal, labelled by the title and described by the description.
- Focus moves into the dialog on open and is restored to the trigger on close.
- Background content is scroll-locked while the dialog is open.

---

# Tooltip

A short contextual hint with accessible trigger association and collision handling.

## Usage

```js
import { Tooltip, TooltipProvider, IconButton } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
// Mount once near the app root so tooltips share open-delay grouping
<TooltipProvider>
  <App />
</TooltipProvider>

// Per-instance delayDuration overrides the provider when needed
<Tooltip content="Refresh search results">
  <IconButton label="Refresh results">
    <Search />
  </IconButton>
</Tooltip>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

An unfamiliar icon or abbreviated label needs a brief hint.
### Avoid when

The user must read or interact with the content.
### Behavior

Hover and focus reveal a short non-interactive description.
### Responsive

Never rely on hover alone; provide a visible label on touch layouts.

## Examples

### Icon button hint

Tooltips label icon-only controls on hover and keyboard focus. Click the icon to expand the search field.

```jsx
import { useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'
import { IconButton, Input, Tooltip } from '@kryv/teal'
import { Search, X } from 'lucide-react'

export function TooltipDemo() {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
  const inputRef = useRef(null)

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!open) return undefined
    const frame = requestAnimationFrame(() => inputRef.current?.focus())
    return () => cancelAnimationFrame(frame)
  }, [open])

  return (
    <div
      className={`relative flex items-center transition-[width] duration-200 ease-out motion-reduce:transition-none ${
        open ? 'w-64' : 'w-10'
      }`}
    >
      {open ? (
        <div className="flex h-10 w-full items-center gap-1 rounded-full border border-outline-variant/40 bg-surface pl-3 pr-1 shadow-sm">
          <Search aria-hidden="true" className="size-4 shrink-0 text-on-surface-variant" />
          <Input
            ref={inputRef}
            aria-label="Search"
            placeholder="Search..."
            onKeyDown={(event) => {
              if (event.key === 'Escape') setOpen(false)
            }}
            className="h-10 min-h-0 flex-1 rounded-none border-transparent bg-transparent px-2 py-0 text-sm shadow-none focus-visible:border-transparent focus-visible:ring-0"
          />
          <IconButton label="Close search" size="sm" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>
            <X />
          </IconButton>
        </div>
      ) : (
        <Tooltip content="Search" delayDuration={0}>
          <IconButton label="Open search" onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>
            <Search />
          </IconButton>
        </Tooltip>
      )}
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Pure hover and focus hint

Use Tooltip for short, non-interactive context around an unfamiliar icon.

```jsx
import { IconButton, Tooltip } from '@kryv/teal'
import { Info } from 'lucide-react'

export function TooltipPureDemo() {
  return (
    <Tooltip content="Only visible context, never an action" delayDuration={0}>
      <IconButton label="More information">
        <Info />
      </IconButton>
    </Tooltip>
  )
}
```

### Action context

Keep the hint short when it sits beside an unfamiliar product action.

```jsx
import { IconButton, Tooltip } from '@kryv/teal'
import { CircleHelp } from 'lucide-react'

export function TooltipActionsDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
      <Tooltip content="Changes apply to everyone in this workspace" delayDuration={0}>
        <IconButton label="Workspace sharing help"><CircleHelp /></IconButton>
      </Tooltip>
      <span className="text-sm text-on-surface-variant">Workspace sharing</span>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Tooltip

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` (required) | `ReactElement<unknown, string \| JSXElementConstructor<any>>` | - | Element the tooltip describes; receives trigger props automatically. |
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional classes merged onto the tooltip content. |
| `content` (required) | `ReactNode` | - | Text rendered inside the tooltip. |
| `delayDuration` | `number` | - | Per-instance open delay in milliseconds; overrides the nearest TooltipProvider. |
| `side` | `enum` | `top` | Preferred side of the trigger for the tooltip. |

### TooltipProvider

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `delayDuration` | `number` | `300` | Open delay in milliseconds shared by the grouped tooltips; moving between triggers skips the delay. |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Focus` | Shows the tooltip when its trigger receives keyboard focus. |
| `Escape` | Dismisses the tooltip. |

- The trigger is described by the tooltip through aria-describedby.
- Tooltips are for short hints only; never place interactive content inside them.
- Wrap the app in TooltipProvider once so tooltips share open-delay grouping; moving between triggers then skips the delay.

---

# Menu

A structured action menu with keyboard navigation, disabled items, icons, and danger styling.

## Usage

```js
import { Menu, IconButton } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Menu
  trigger={<IconButton label="Project actions"><MoreVertical /></IconButton>}
  items={[
    { id: 'settings', label: 'Settings', onSelect: () => undefined },
    { id: 'archive', label: 'Archive', variant: 'danger', onSelect: () => undefined },
  ]}
/>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

Several related actions belong behind one trigger.
### Avoid when

The actions should remain visible for frequent workflows.
### Behavior

Keyboard navigation and dismissal are managed by Radix.
### Responsive

Keep destructive actions separated and easy to reach on touch.

## Examples

### Project actions

Items support icons, separators, and a danger variant for destructive actions.

```jsx
import { IconButton, Menu } from '@kryv/teal'
import { Archive, MoreVertical, Settings } from 'lucide-react'

export function MenuDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  return (
    <Menu
      trigger={
        <IconButton label={exampleIndex ? 'Additional actions' : 'Project actions'}>
          <MoreVertical />
        </IconButton>
      }
      items={[
        { id: 'settings', label: 'Settings', icon: <Settings />, onSelect: () => undefined },
        {
          id: 'archive',
          label: 'Archive',
          icon: <Archive />,
          variant: 'danger',
          separatorBefore: true,
          onSelect: () => undefined,
        },
      ]}
    />
  )
}
```

### Separated destructive action

Keep destructive actions at the end of the menu behind a separator.

```jsx
import { IconButton, Menu } from '@kryv/teal'
import { Archive, MoreVertical, Settings } from 'lucide-react'

export function MenuDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  return (
    <Menu
      trigger={
        <IconButton label={exampleIndex ? 'Additional actions' : 'Project actions'}>
          <MoreVertical />
        </IconButton>
      }
      items={[
        { id: 'settings', label: 'Settings', icon: <Settings />, onSelect: () => undefined },
        {
          id: 'archive',
          label: 'Archive',
          icon: <Archive />,
          variant: 'danger',
          separatorBefore: true,
          onSelect: () => undefined,
        },
      ]}
    />
  )
}
```

### Keyboard action menu

Menus preserve arrow-key navigation and Escape dismissal.

```jsx
import { IconButton, Menu } from '@kryv/teal'
import { Archive, MoreVertical, Settings } from 'lucide-react'

export function MenuDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  return (
    <Menu
      trigger={
        <IconButton label={exampleIndex ? 'Additional actions' : 'Project actions'}>
          <MoreVertical />
        </IconButton>
      }
      items={[
        { id: 'settings', label: 'Settings', icon: <Settings />, onSelect: () => undefined },
        {
          id: 'archive',
          label: 'Archive',
          icon: <Archive />,
          variant: 'danger',
          separatorBefore: true,
          onSelect: () => undefined,
        },
      ]}
    />
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Menu

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `align` | `enum` | `end` | Horizontal alignment of the menu relative to the trigger. |
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional classes merged onto the menu content. |
| `items` (required) | `MenuItem[]` | - | Items rendered in the menu. |
| `label` | `string` | - | Accessible name for the menu. |
| `modal` | `boolean` | `false` | Traps focus and blocks outside interaction while open. Defaults to false. |
| `trigger` (required) | `ReactElement<unknown, string \| JSXElementConstructor<any>>` | - | Element that opens the menu; receives trigger props automatically. |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Enter` + `Space` + `Arrow Down` | Opens the menu and highlights the first item. |
| `Arrow Up` | Opens the menu and highlights the last item. |
| `Arrow Up` + `Arrow Down` | Moves the highlight between items, skipping disabled ones. |
| `Home` + `End` | Highlights the first or last item. |
| `Typing` | Typeahead jumps to the first matching item. |
| `Enter` + `Space` | Activates the highlighted item and closes the menu. |
| `Escape` + `Tab` | Closes the menu and returns focus to the trigger. |

- Renders the ARIA menu pattern with menuitem roles and roving highlight.
- Icons are decorative; every item keeps a text label.
- Set modal to trap focus and block outside interaction while the menu is open.

---

# Popover

An anchored surface for arbitrary controls and supplemental content.

## Usage

```js
import { Popover, Button, Checkbox } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Popover label="Filter projects" trigger={<Button variant="secondary">Filters</Button>}>
  <div className="grid gap-3">
    <Checkbox label="Active only" defaultChecked />
    <Button size="sm">Apply filters</Button>
  </div>
</Popover>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

Supplemental controls should stay anchored to a trigger.
### Avoid when

The content is a blocking task or a simple one-line hint.
### Behavior

Focus returns to the trigger after dismissal.
### Responsive

Keep panels within the viewport and avoid overly wide forms.

## Examples

### Filter panel

Popover anchors interactive content to a trigger with collision-aware placement.

```jsx
import { Button, Checkbox, Popover } from '@kryv/teal'
import { Filter } from 'lucide-react'

export function PopoverDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  return (
    <Popover
      label="Filter projects"
      trigger={
        <Button variant="secondary">
          <Filter /> {exampleIndex ? 'Refine projects' : 'Filters'}
        </Button>
      }
      align="start"
    >
      <div className="grid gap-3">
        <h3 className="font-headline font-bold">Filter projects</h3>
        <Checkbox label="Active only" defaultChecked />
        <Button size="sm">Apply filters</Button>
      </div>
    </Popover>
  )
}
```

### Inline filters

Keep a small set of filters anchored to the toolbar that owns them.

```jsx
import { Button, Checkbox, Popover } from '@kryv/teal'
import { Filter } from 'lucide-react'

export function PopoverDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  return (
    <Popover
      label="Filter projects"
      trigger={
        <Button variant="secondary">
          <Filter /> {exampleIndex ? 'Refine projects' : 'Filters'}
        </Button>
      }
      align="start"
    >
      <div className="grid gap-3">
        <h3 className="font-headline font-bold">Filter projects</h3>
        <Checkbox label="Active only" defaultChecked />
        <Button size="sm">Apply filters</Button>
      </div>
    </Popover>
  )
}
```

### Supplemental controls

Use a popover for controls that do not deserve a full route or dialog.

```jsx
import { Button, Checkbox, Popover } from '@kryv/teal'
import { Filter } from 'lucide-react'

export function PopoverDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  return (
    <Popover
      label="Filter projects"
      trigger={
        <Button variant="secondary">
          <Filter /> {exampleIndex ? 'Refine projects' : 'Filters'}
        </Button>
      }
      align="start"
    >
      <div className="grid gap-3">
        <h3 className="font-headline font-bold">Filter projects</h3>
        <Checkbox label="Active only" defaultChecked />
        <Button size="sm">Apply filters</Button>
      </div>
    </Popover>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Popover

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `align` | `enum` | `center` | Horizontal alignment of the content relative to the trigger. |
| `children` (required) | `ReactNode` | - | Content rendered inside the popover surface. |
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional classes merged onto the popover content. |
| `defaultOpen` | `boolean` | - | Initial open state when uncontrolled. |
| `label` (required) | `string` | - | Accessible name for the dialog-like popover surface. |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void` | - | Called when the popover opens or closes. |
| `open` | `boolean` | - | Controlled open state. |
| `side` | `enum` | `bottom` | Side of the trigger the content opens on. |
| `trigger` (required) | `ReactElement<unknown, string \| JSXElementConstructor<any>>` | - | Element that toggles the popover; receives trigger props automatically. |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Escape` | Closes the popover and returns focus to the trigger. |
| `Tab` | Moves focus into the popover content, then back into the page flow. |

- The trigger exposes aria-expanded and aria-controls for the popover content.
- Focus is not trapped; popovers suit small control panels, not multi-step flows.
- Placement flips automatically to stay inside the viewport.

---

# Toast

Imperative, announced feedback with semantic variants, optional actions, and dismissal.

## Usage

```js
import { Toaster, toast } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
// Mount once near the app root
<Toaster />

// Call toast() from anywhere
toast({ title: 'Changes saved', variant: 'success' })
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

A completed or failed action needs brief asynchronous feedback.
### Avoid when

The message is required to continue or must be read in context.
### Behavior

Toaster announces messages and supports timed or manual dismissal.
### Responsive

Position toasts away from mobile browser controls and safe areas.

## Examples

### Saving feedback

Call toast() from anywhere once a Toaster is mounted near the app root.

```jsx
import { Button, toast } from '@kryv/teal'

export function ToastDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  return (
    <Button
      onClick={() =>
      toast({ title: exampleIndex ? 'Review saved' : 'Changes saved', description: 'Your settings are up to date.', variant: 'success' })
      }
    >
      {exampleIndex ? 'Show review toast' : 'Show toast'}
    </Button>
  )
}
```

### Failure feedback

Use a danger variant for a failed action and keep the recovery path in context.

```jsx
import { Button, toast } from '@kryv/teal'

export function ToastDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  return (
    <Button
      onClick={() =>
      toast({ title: exampleIndex ? 'Review saved' : 'Changes saved', description: 'Your settings are up to date.', variant: 'success' })
      }
    >
      {exampleIndex ? 'Show review toast' : 'Show toast'}
    </Button>
  )
}
```

### Undo feedback

Offer a short action when users may want to reverse a completed operation.

```jsx
import { Button, toast } from '@kryv/teal'

export function ToastDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  return (
    <Button
      onClick={() =>
      toast({ title: exampleIndex ? 'Review saved' : 'Changes saved', description: 'Your settings are up to date.', variant: 'success' })
      }
    >
      {exampleIndex ? 'Show review toast' : 'Show toast'}
    </Button>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Toaster

_This module forwards native element properties._

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `F8` | Moves focus to the toast viewport so toasts can be reviewed and dismissed. |
| `Escape` | Dismisses the focused toast. |
| `Swipe right` | Dismisses a toast on touch devices. |

- Toasts are announced through a live region as they appear.
- Actions render as real buttons with accessible labels.
- Pass duration: Infinity for critical messages that must be dismissed manually.

---

# Empty State

An explanatory empty result with an optional action and SVG icon.

## Usage

```js
import { EmptyState, Button } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<EmptyState
  title="No reports"
  description="Create a report to begin tracking results."
  action={<Button>Create report</Button>}
/>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

A product surface has no results or has not been configured.
### Avoid when

Content is merely loading or filtered temporarily.
### Behavior

Explain what happened and give one clear next action when useful.
### Responsive

Keep the message readable and center the action beneath it.

## Examples

### First-run

Pair a short explanation with a single primary action.

```jsx
import { Button, EmptyState } from '@kryv/teal'

export function EmptyStateDemo() {
  return (
    <EmptyState
      title="No reports"
      description="Create a report to begin tracking results."
      action={<Button>Create report</Button>}
      className="w-full max-w-lg"
    />
  )
}
```

### Filtered empty result

Explain that filters produced no results and offer a way to adjust them.

```jsx
import { Button, EmptyState } from '@kryv/teal'

export function EmptyStateDemo() {
  return (
    <EmptyState
      title="No reports"
      description="Create a report to begin tracking results."
      action={<Button>Create report</Button>}
      className="w-full max-w-lg"
    />
  )
}
```

## Interface

### EmptyState

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `action` | `ReactNode` | - | Call to action rendered below the description. |
| `description` | `ReactNode` | - | Supporting text that explains the empty state. |
| `icon` | `ReactNode` | - | Icon rendered above the title. |
| `title` (required) | `ReactNode` | - | Short heading for the empty state. |
| `titleAs` | `enum` | `"h3"` | Heading element used for the title; defaults to 'h3'. Adjust to fit the page outline. |

## Accessibility

- The title renders as an h3 by default; adjust titleAs so the empty state fits the page outline.
- The action is a real button or link; keep to a single primary action.

---

# Loading

Named progress and loading treatments for local, skeleton, and full-surface states.

## Usage

```js
import { Spinner, Progress, Skeleton, LoadingState } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Spinner label="Saving" />
<Progress label="Import progress" value={64} />
<Skeleton className="h-4 w-40" />
<LoadingState label="Loading reports" />
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

Users need feedback while content or work is in progress.
### Avoid when

The operation is instant or no meaningful progress exists.
### Behavior

Use Spinner for local work, Skeleton for layout, and Progress for measurable work.
### Responsive

Prefer local indicators so small screens retain useful content.

## Examples

### Loading treatments

Spinner and Progress for active work, Skeleton for layout placeholders, LoadingState for regions.

```jsx
import { LoadingState, Progress, Skeleton, Spinner } from '@kryv/teal'

export function LoadingDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-lg gap-5">
      <div className="flex items-center gap-5">
        <Spinner label="Loading example" />
        <Progress label="Import progress" value={64} />
      </div>
      <Skeleton className="h-20 w-full" />
      <LoadingState label="Loading reports" className="min-h-32" />
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Skeleton region

Reserve the eventual layout with Skeleton when content shape is known.

```jsx
import { LoadingState, Progress, Skeleton, Spinner } from '@kryv/teal'

export function LoadingDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-lg gap-5">
      <div className="flex items-center gap-5">
        <Spinner label="Loading example" />
        <Progress label="Import progress" value={64} />
      </div>
      <Skeleton className="h-20 w-full" />
      <LoadingState label="Loading reports" className="min-h-32" />
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### LoadingState

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `label` | `string` | `Loading` | Accessible label announced by screen readers while the region is loading. |

### Spinner

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `label` | `string` | `Loading` | Accessible label announced by screen readers. |
| `size` | `enum` | `md` | Diameter of the spinner. |

### Skeleton

_This module forwards native element properties._

### Progress

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `label` | `string` | `Loading` | Accessible label describing what the progress bar measures. |
| `asChild` | `boolean` | - | Merges the component onto its child element instead of rendering the default element (Radix Slot). |
| `value` | `number \| null` | `0` | Current progress, clamped between 0 and max. |
| `max` | `number` | `100` | Maximum value used to compute the filled percentage. |

## Accessibility

- Spinner and LoadingState render role="status" with an accessible label, announced once to screen readers.
- Progress exposes aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, and aria-valuemax through the Radix progress primitive.
- Skeleton is aria-hidden because it duplicates content that is about to load.
- All motion respects prefers-reduced-motion.

---

# Alert

An inline feedback banner with semantic variants, an optional title, and dismissal.

## Usage

```js
import { Alert } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Alert variant="warning" title="Payment method expiring">
  The workspace card ends in 04/25. Update billing details to avoid interruption.
</Alert>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

Feedback must stay visible in context until it is read or dismissed.
### Avoid when

A brief confirmation is enough; use a toast for transient feedback.
### Behavior

Danger renders role="alert" for immediate announcement; other variants render role="status".
### Responsive

Let the body text wrap and keep the title to a short phrase.

## Examples

### Variants

Semantic variants pair a standard icon with a matching surface treatment.

```jsx
import { Alert } from '@kryv/teal'

export function AlertDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  if (exampleIndex === 1) {
    return (
      <Alert
        variant="warning"
        title="Payment method expiring"
        onDismiss={() => undefined}
        className="w-full max-w-lg"
      >
        The workspace card ends in 04/25. Update billing details to avoid interruption.
      </Alert>
    )
  }
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-lg gap-3">
      <Alert variant="success" title="Report published">
        The quarterly security report is now available to all workspace members.
      </Alert>
      <Alert variant="danger" title="Sign-in blocked">
        We stopped a sign-in attempt from an unrecognized device.
      </Alert>
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Dismissible

Pass onDismiss to render a close button for feedback the user can clear.

```jsx
import { Alert } from '@kryv/teal'

export function AlertDemo({ exampleIndex = 0 }) {
  if (exampleIndex === 1) {
    return (
      <Alert
        variant="warning"
        title="Payment method expiring"
        onDismiss={() => undefined}
        className="w-full max-w-lg"
      >
        The workspace card ends in 04/25. Update billing details to avoid interruption.
      </Alert>
    )
  }
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full max-w-lg gap-3">
      <Alert variant="success" title="Report published">
        The quarterly security report is now available to all workspace members.
      </Alert>
      <Alert variant="danger" title="Sign-in blocked">
        We stopped a sign-in attempt from an unrecognized device.
      </Alert>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Alert

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `icon` | `ReactNode` | - | Icon override; defaults to the variant's standard icon. Always hidden from assistive technology. |
| `onDismiss` | `() => void` | - | Renders a dismiss button and calls this when it is pressed. |
| `title` | `ReactNode` | - | Bold leading text of the alert. |
| `variant` | `"neutral" \| "info" \| "success" \| "warning" \| "danger" \| null` | `"info"` | Semantic treatment of the banner. |

## Accessibility

- Danger renders role="alert" so the message is announced immediately; other variants render role="status".
- The variant icon is decorative and hidden from assistive technology; meaning is carried by the text.
- The dismiss control is a labeled button in the normal tab order.

---

# Tabs

Keyboard-navigable content switching through a compact item interface.

## Usage

```js
import { Tabs } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<Tabs
  aria-label="Account sections"
  defaultValue="profile"
  items={[
    { value: 'profile', label: 'Profile', content: <ProfilePanel /> },
    { value: 'billing', label: 'Billing', content: <BillingPanel /> },
  ]}
/>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

Related views share a context and users switch between them.
### Avoid when

Views need independent URLs or a long sequence of steps.
### Behavior

Arrow keys move between tabs and the active panel is announced.
### Responsive

Allow tab labels to scroll rather than wrap into ambiguous rows.

## Examples

### Sections

Tabs follow the ARIA authoring practices keyboard pattern out of the box.

```jsx
import { Tabs } from '@kryv/teal'

export function TabsDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="w-full max-w-xl">
      <Tabs
        aria-label="Project details"
        defaultValue="overview"
        items={[
          { value: 'overview', label: 'Overview', content: <p className="text-sm">Project overview content</p> },
          { value: 'activity', label: 'Activity', content: <p className="text-sm">Recent project activity</p> },
          { value: 'access', label: 'Access', content: <p className="text-sm">Workspace access settings</p> },
        ]}
      />
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Profile sections

Use tabs for peer views that share the same route context.

```jsx
import { Tabs } from '@kryv/teal'

export function TabsDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="w-full max-w-xl">
      <Tabs
        aria-label="Project details"
        defaultValue="overview"
        items={[
          { value: 'overview', label: 'Overview', content: <p className="text-sm">Project overview content</p> },
          { value: 'activity', label: 'Activity', content: <p className="text-sm">Recent project activity</p> },
          { value: 'access', label: 'Access', content: <p className="text-sm">Workspace access settings</p> },
        ]}
      />
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Responsive tab list

Long tab labels remain reachable through horizontal scrolling.

```jsx
import { Tabs } from '@kryv/teal'

export function TabsResponsiveDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="w-full max-w-xl">
      <Tabs
        aria-label="Report sections"
        defaultValue="summary"
        items={[
          { value: 'summary', label: 'Executive summary', content: <p className="text-sm">A concise summary stays visible first.</p> },
          { value: 'evidence', label: 'Evidence and activity', content: <p className="text-sm">Detailed evidence is available without changing route context.</p> },
          { value: 'access', label: 'Access policy', content: <p className="text-sm">Permissions remain a peer view of this report.</p> },
        ]}
      />
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### Tabs

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `aria-label` (required) | `string` | - | Accessible name for the tab list. |
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional classes merged onto the tabs root. |
| `defaultValue` | `string` | - | Initially active tab when uncontrolled. |
| `items` (required) | `TabItem[]` | - | Tabs rendered by the component. |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: string) => void` | - | Called with the new value when the active tab changes. |
| `value` | `string` | - | Controlled active tab value. |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Arrow Left` + `Arrow Right` | Moves between tabs and activates them. |
| `Home` + `End` | Activates the first or last tab. |
| `Tab` | Moves focus from the active tab into its panel. |

- Implements the ARIA tabs pattern with tablist, tab, and tabpanel roles.
- aria-label on the tab list describes what the tabs switch between.
- Disabled tabs are skipped during arrow-key navigation.

---

# Pagination

A controlled page navigator with compact ranges and unavailable directions.

## Usage

```js
import { Pagination } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
const [page, setPage] = useState(1)

<Pagination page={page} pageCount={12} onPageChange={setPage} />
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

A large collection is split into stable pages.
### Avoid when

Users need continuous search, sorting, or infinite history.
### Behavior

The page is controlled by the consumer and unavailable directions are disabled.
### Responsive

Keep controls large enough for touch and preserve the current page label.

## Examples

### Controlled pages

Pagination is fully controlled through page and onPageChange.

```jsx
import { useState } from 'react'
import { Pagination } from '@kryv/teal'

export function PaginationDemo() {
  const [page, setPage] = useState(3)
  return <Pagination page={page} pageCount={8} onPageChange={setPage} />
}
```

### Boundary pages

Disable previous and next controls at the collection boundaries.

```jsx
import { useState } from 'react'
import { Pagination } from '@kryv/teal'

export function PaginationDemo() {
  const [page, setPage] = useState(3)
  return <Pagination page={page} pageCount={8} onPageChange={setPage} />
}
```

## Interface

### Pagination

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `className` | `string` | - | Additional classes merged onto the nav element. |
| `label` | `string` | `Pagination` | Accessible name for the navigation landmark. |
| `onPageChange` (required) | `(page: number) => void` | - | Called with the next page when the user navigates. |
| `page` (required) | `number` | - | Current page, one-based. |
| `pageCount` (required) | `number` | - | Total number of pages. |

## Accessibility

| Keys | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Tab` | Moves focus through the page controls. |
| `Enter` + `Space` | Activates the focused page control. |

- Renders a nav element with an accessible label.
- The current page is marked with aria-current="page".
- Unavailable directions render as disabled buttons rather than being hidden.

---

# Page Header

A responsive page title, supporting text, and action area.

## Usage

```js
import { PageHeader, Button } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<PageHeader
  title="Workspace settings"
  subtitle="Manage security and notifications"
  actions={<Button>Save changes</Button>}
/>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

A route needs a consistent title, context, and primary actions.
### Avoid when

The content is a small inline section without route-level actions.
### Behavior

Actions remain aligned with the title and wrap below it when needed.
### Responsive

Let actions wrap naturally below the heading at narrow widths.

## Examples

### Settings header

Actions wrap below the title on narrow screens automatically.

```jsx
import { Button, PageHeader } from '@kryv/teal'

export function PageHeaderDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="w-full">
      <PageHeader
        title="Workspace settings"
        subtitle="Manage security and notifications"
        actions={<Button>Save changes</Button>}
      />
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Responsive actions

Let actions wrap beneath the title on narrow screens.

```jsx
import { Button, PageHeader } from '@kryv/teal'

export function PageHeaderDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="w-full">
      <PageHeader
        title="Workspace settings"
        subtitle="Manage security and notifications"
        actions={<Button>Save changes</Button>}
      />
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Interface

### PageHeader

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `actions` | `ReactNode` | - | Actions rendered beside the title; wraps below on narrow screens. |
| `subtitle` | `ReactNode` | - | Supporting text rendered under the title. |
| `title` (required) | `ReactNode` | - | Page title rendered as a heading. |
| `titleAs` | `enum` | `"h1"` | Heading element used for the title; defaults to 'h1'. Adjust to fit the page outline. |

## Accessibility

- The title renders as an h1 by default; set titleAs to fit the page outline and keep one h1 per page.
- Actions are regular buttons and links with standard focus order.

---

# Vertical Nav

A compound vertical navigation with icon-rail and full-text modes.

## Usage

```js
import { VerticalNav, VerticalNavBrand, VerticalNavList, VerticalNavSection, VerticalNavItem, VerticalNavFooter } from '@kryv/teal'
```

```jsx
<VerticalNav mode="rail">
  <VerticalNavBrand>...</VerticalNavBrand>
  <VerticalNavList>
    <VerticalNavSection label="Workspace">
      <VerticalNavItem active icon={<LayoutDashboard />}>Overview</VerticalNavItem>
    </VerticalNavSection>
  </VerticalNavList>
  <VerticalNavFooter>...</VerticalNavFooter>
</VerticalNav>
```

## Design guidance

### Use when

An application needs persistent section navigation.
### Avoid when

There are only a few inline links or a short wizard.
### Behavior

Rail mode expands on hover or focus and item active state sets aria-current.
### Responsive

Use full navigation in a drawer on mobile and close it after route changes.

## Examples

### Rail and full modes

Rail mode collapses to an icon strip and expands on hover or keyboard focus. Full mode shows labels always.

```jsx
import { FolderKanban, LayoutDashboard, Settings, Users } from 'lucide-react'
import { VerticalNav, VerticalNavBrand, VerticalNavFooter, VerticalNavItem, VerticalNavList, VerticalNavSection } from '@kryv/teal'

export function VerticalNavDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full gap-4 sm:grid-cols-2">
      <div className="relative h-80 overflow-hidden rounded-xl border border-outline-variant/30">
        <VerticalNav mode="rail" className="absolute">
          <VerticalNavBrand>
            <span className="flex w-16 items-center justify-center">
              <span className="flex size-9 items-center justify-center rounded-xl bg-primary text-on-primary">
                <LayoutDashboard className="size-5" />
              </span>
            </span>
          </VerticalNavBrand>
          <VerticalNavList>
            <VerticalNavSection label="Workspace">
              <VerticalNavItem active icon={<LayoutDashboard className="size-5" />}>
                Overview
              </VerticalNavItem>
              <VerticalNavItem icon={<FolderKanban className="size-5" />}>Projects</VerticalNavItem>
              <VerticalNavItem icon={<Users className="size-5" />}>Team</VerticalNavItem>
            </VerticalNavSection>
          </VerticalNavList>
          <VerticalNavFooter>
            <VerticalNavItem icon={<Settings className="size-5" />}>Settings</VerticalNavItem>
          </VerticalNavFooter>
        </VerticalNav>
      </div>

      <div className="relative h-80 overflow-hidden rounded-xl border border-outline-variant/30">
        <VerticalNav mode="full" className="absolute">
          <VerticalNavBrand>
            <span className="flex w-16 items-center justify-center">
              <span className="flex size-9 items-center justify-center rounded-xl bg-primary text-on-primary">
                <LayoutDashboard className="size-5" />
              </span>
            </span>
            <span className="font-headline text-lg font-extrabold leading-none">Teal</span>
          </VerticalNavBrand>
          <VerticalNavList>
            <VerticalNavSection label="Workspace">
              <VerticalNavItem active icon={<LayoutDashboard className="size-5" />}>
                Overview
              </VerticalNavItem>
              <VerticalNavItem icon={<FolderKanban className="size-5" />}>Projects</VerticalNavItem>
              <VerticalNavItem icon={<Users className="size-5" />}>Team</VerticalNavItem>
            </VerticalNavSection>
          </VerticalNavList>
          <VerticalNavFooter>
            <VerticalNavItem icon={<Settings className="size-5" />}>Settings</VerticalNavItem>
          </VerticalNavFooter>
        </VerticalNav>
      </div>
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Application shell

Compose a persistent rail with a full navigation drawer for responsive products.

```jsx
import { FolderKanban, LayoutDashboard, Settings, Users } from 'lucide-react'
import { VerticalNav, VerticalNavBrand, VerticalNavFooter, VerticalNavItem, VerticalNavList, VerticalNavSection } from '@kryv/teal'

export function VerticalNavDemo() {
  return (
    <div className="grid w-full gap-4 sm:grid-cols-2">
      <div className="relative h-80 overflow-hidden rounded-xl border border-outline-variant/30">
        <VerticalNav mode="rail" className="absolute">
          <VerticalNavBrand>
            <span className="flex w-16 items-center justify-center">
              <span className="flex size-9 items-center justify-center rounded-xl bg-primary text-on-primary">
                <LayoutDashboard className="size-5" />
              </span>
            </span>
          </VerticalNavBrand>
          <VerticalNavList>
            <VerticalNavSection label="Workspace">
              <VerticalNavItem active icon={<LayoutDashboard className="size-5" />}>
                Overview
              </VerticalNavItem>
              <VerticalNavItem icon={<FolderKanban className="size-5" />}>Projects</VerticalNavItem>
              <VerticalNavItem icon={<Users className="size-5" />}>Team</VerticalNavItem>
            </VerticalNavSection>
          </VerticalNavList>
          <VerticalNavFooter>
            <VerticalNavItem icon={<Settings className="size-5" />}>Settings</VerticalNavItem>
          </VerticalNavFooter>
        </VerticalNav>
      </div>

      <div className="relative h-80 overflow-hidden rounded-xl border border-outline-variant/30">
        <VerticalNav mode="full" className="absolute">
          <VerticalNavBrand>
            <span className="flex w-16 items-center justify-center">
              <span className="flex size-9 items-center justify-center rounded-xl bg-primary text-on-primary">
                <LayoutDashboard className="size-5" />
              </span>
            </span>
```
